articles.title: ASPECT RATIO, LOCKED
articles.summary: Figma now lets users lock the aspect ratio of frames, shapes, images, videos, vectors, and text for proportional scaling. This replaces Constrain proportions, allowing resizing from the properties panel or canvas. Previously constrained objects require manually applying the new Lock aspect ratio feature.

articles.title: THE YOUTUBE LOGO CHANGED COLOUR, AND PEOPLE ARE ONLY JUST NOTICING
articles.summary: The YouTube logo has undergone a subtle color change, with the logo itself becoming slightly pinker. This change occurred in October, but people are only starting to notice it now. The change departs from the platform's usual release of unique edition designs for specific events or holidays.

articles.title: GOOGLE IS ADDING DIGITAL WATERMARKS TO IMAGES EDITED WITH MAGIC EDITOR AI
articles.summary: AI-edited images in Google Photos now include a digital watermark, starting with the Reimagine feature in Magic Editor on Pixel 9. Developed by DeepMind, SynthID embeds watermarks without altering images. This approach enhances transparency as AI-generated content becomes more realistic, though minor edits may not always trigger marking.

articles.title: SIMPLIFY YOUR PRODUCT DESIGN — BORROW FAMILIAR PATTERNS
articles.summary: Familiar design patterns make products feel intuitive. WhatsApp mimicked phone OS layouts to ensure ease of use. Consistent UI elements build predictability, while strategic button placement enhances usability. Even complex AI chatbots succeed by borrowing familiar messaging interfaces, proving that simplicity comes from aligning with users' habits and expectations.

articles.title: THE FLYWHEEL OF BRAND
articles.summary: Marty Neumeier's The Brand Flip argued that consumers now shape brands. Since 2015, short-form video, personal branding, product-as-content, and rising consumer expectations have intensified this shift. The Flywheel of the Brand model emphasizes products as the key driver of trust, brand-building, and differentiation, replacing traditional marketing-heavy approaches.

articles.title: IT'S TIME FOR DESIGN TO THINK LESS AND FEEL MORE
BEAUTIFUL FREE STOCK PHOTOS
articles.summary: New CC0 images added daily, free from copyright restrictions.

articles.title: A SYSTEMATIC APPROACH TO TECHNICAL DOCUMENTATION AUTHORING
articles.summary: Diátaxis prescribes approaches to content, architecture, and form that emerge from a systematic approach to understanding the needs of documentation users.

articles.title: CHAT INTERFACES AND DECLARING INTENT
articles.summary: AI-powered chat interfaces have revolutionized user intent capture, evolving from information retrieval to manipulation and action execution. Unlike past systems that required users to adapt, today's interfaces understand natural language and handle diverse requests. As AI advances, UI design should minimize unnecessary constraints, allowing computers to interpret human intent more seamlessly.

articles.title: REDEFINING UX: BEHAVIOR AND ANTICIPATORY DESIGN IN THE AGE OF AI
articles.summary: Technology simplifies life but also causes overload. Anticipatory design, using AI to predict needs, often fails due to poor understanding of behavior. Success requires balancing automation with user control, personalization, and timely interventions, leveraging behavior models like B=MAP, the Transtheoretical Model, and the Nudge Theory while addressing data quality and privacy.

articles.title: DOES THE DESIGN INDUSTRY HAVE A PROBLEM WITH GHOSTING?
articles.summary: Ghosting is a rising issue in the design industry, with 77% of designers reporting it as a growing problem, often leaving them with wasted time and unanswered questions. Factors like a buyer's market, shifting work patterns, and a decline in design's perceived value contribute to this trend, making it both a commercial and emotional challenge.

articles.title: I JUST SAW ADOBE PROJECT CONCEPT IN ACTION, AND IT'S THE BEST USE CASE FOR GEN AI YET
articles.summary: Adobe Project Concept is a browser-based AI-powered mood-boarding tool that enables flexible ideation.

articles.title: THIS DESIGNER TRANSFORMS ROUGH SKETCHES INTO ACTUAL FURNITURE… QUITE LITERALLY!
articles.summary: Jinil Park's Drawing Series transforms sketch-like doodles into functional steel furniture.
